name: Voyager 1
description: First human-made object to enter interstellar space (2012). Carries the Golden Record and continues to transmit science data from beyond the heliopause.

name: Voyager 2
description: Only spacecraft to have flown by all four gas giants (Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune). Crossed the heliopause in 2018.

name: Pioneer 10
description: First spacecraft to traverse the asteroid belt and first to make direct observations of Jupiter. Last signal received Jan 2003.

name: Pioneer 11
description: First spacecraft to encounter Saturn and the second to fly through the asteroid belt and past Jupiter.

name: Galileo
description: First spacecraft to orbit Jupiter (1995). Dropped a probe into Jupiter's atmosphere and conducted 34 orbits before intentional impact.

name: Cassini-Huygens
description: Saturn orbiter and Huygens Titan lander. 13-year mission redefined our understanding of Saturn, its rings, and its moons.

name: New Horizons
description: First spacecraft to fly by Pluto (2015) and Kuiper Belt object Arrokoth (2019). Currently exploring the Kuiper Belt.

name: Juno
description: Jupiter polar orbiter studying the planet's atmosphere, gravity, magnetic field, and origin. Extended mission includes flybys of Galilean moons.

name: Parker Solar Probe
description: Closest spacecraft to the Sun ever (~6.2M km). Studies the solar corona, solar wind origin, and magnetic fields.

name: BepiColombo
description: Dual-spacecraft mission to Mercury. Currently in cruise phase with multiple gravity assists. Mercury orbit insertion planned 2026.

name: Europa Clipper
description: Will conduct 49 flybys of Jupiter's icy moon Europa to investigate the subsurface ocean and search for conditions suitable for life.

name: JUICE
description: Jupiter Icy Moons Explorer. Will study Ganymede, Callisto, and Europa in detail. Arrives at Jupiter July 2031.

name: Artemis II
description: First crewed lunar mission since Apollo 17 (1972). Free-return trajectory around the Moon with closest approach ~6,600 km above the far side.
